fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Alaska Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as an occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Alaska - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity alaska, how to transfer occupational therapist license to alaska, occupational therapist compact alaska, alaska occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Alaska PT & OT Board
board.parent_dept: Division of Corporations, Business and Professional Licensing

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT)

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Alaska.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Register for a MyLicense - Alaska Account
steps.description: Create an account on the MyLicense - Alaska portal. Provide your personal details, create a unique username and password, and verify your email address to activate your account.

steps.title: Complete and Submit Online Application
steps.description: Log into your MyLicense - Alaska account, select the 'Occupational Therapist License by Endorsement' or 'Occupational Therapist License by Examination' application, and accurately fill out all required sections. Ensure all information is correct before submission.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload or arrange for submission of all necessary supporting documents, such as official transcripts showing an occupational therapy degree, verification of NBCOT certification, and any required affidavits, directly through the MyLicense - Alaska portal or as instructed.

steps.title: Arrange for License Verification
steps.description: If applying by endorsement, contact all states where you have held an OT license to request direct verification of your license(s) to the Alaska Board of Occupational Therapy. This is typically done through a state's licensing portal or a mailed form.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee as part of the online application process through the MyLicense - Alaska portal using a credit card.

steps.title: Board Review and Licensure Decision
steps.description: Allow time for the Alaska Board of Occupational Therapy to review your complete application and all supporting documents. The Board will notify you of any deficiencies or the final licensure decision through your MyLicense - Alaska account or by mail.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Alaska offers a temporary occupational therapy permit for new graduates who have completed an ACOTE-accredited program and are awaiting their NBCOT exam results. The temporary permit allows practice under the direct supervision of a licensed OTR. Contact the Alaska PT & OT Board for current temporary permit requirements and duration limits.
reciprocity_text: Occupational therapists licensed in another state may apply for licensure in Alaska through endorsement. Submit a completed application to the Alaska PT & OT Board, along with official license verification from your current state and proof of active NBCOT certification. Processing typically takes 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: How much does a Alaska OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial application and licensing fee is approximately $350. Check the board website for the latest fee schedule.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Alaska have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers occupational therapist practice in Alaska. To work in Alaska, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Alaska?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Alaska while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Occupational therapists licensed in another state may apply for licensure in Alaska through endorsement. Submit a completed application to the Alaska PT & OT Board, along with official license verification from your current state and proof of active NBCOT certification. Processing typically takes 4 to 6 Weeks.

hero.title: Become an Occupational Therapist in Alaska
hero.dek: To apply for an Occupational Therapist license in Alaska, applicants must utilize the state's online licensing system, MyLicense - Alaska, to submit their application and required documentation.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Go to the MyLicense - Alaska portal, click on 'Register' to create a new user account. Follow the prompts to enter your personal information, create a username and password, and verify your email. Once registered, log in to access the online application dashboard and select the appropriate application for Occupational Therapist licensure by examination or endorsement.
